published_prior_art.cite: Pearl1995CausalDiagrams: front-door functional at the exclusion/no-direct-path corner
standard compactness plus nested feasible-set endpoint monotonicity, not a novel published kernel

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_frontdoor_multi_mediator: shared-latent non-rectangularity obstacle for a different multi-mediator/proxy PNDE setting; pid_proximal_l2_bridge: proximal endpoint/inference setting, not leaky front-door ACE

published_prior_art.cite: Pearl1995CausalDiagrams gives only lambda=0 point ID; FulcherShpitserMarealleTchetgen2017GeneralizedFrontDoor targets PIIE; DingVanderWeele2016SharpMediationSensitivity treats mediator-outcome confounding, not clean-mediator direct-leak ACE

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_frontdoor_multi_mediator asks for a small rational non-rectangularity witness but does not pose this single-mediator leak-indexed vertex enumeration

published_prior_art.cite: BalkePearl1997Bounds and FinkelsteinAdamsSariaShpitser2020MeasurementError provide LP/discrete-latent templates, not a bilinear shared-U leaky-front-door vertex formula

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_proximal_l2_bridge Open-ended Question 3: endpoint inference for nonsmooth proximal partial-ID maps

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovHongTamer2007ParameterSets, FangSantos2019DirectionallyDifferentiable, and GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023ProxyPartialID provide the inference template but not this endpoint map

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The per-mediator box B_box drops Bayes consistency and the shared w_x tie but still optimizes the ACE formula requiring a marginal pi, so the relaxation is under-specified unless pi is made free, tied to per-equation laws, or otherwise defined.

soundness_flags.label: Open-ended Question 4
soundness_flags.one_line: The closed-form kink threshold lambda* > 0 is stated without a regime condition; boundary laws such as no confounder shift, no active-set switch, or degenerate support need an explicit no-kink convention or exclusion.

soundness_flags.label: Open-ended Question 5
soundness_flags.one_line: The inference question depends on lambda* from Open-ended Question 4, so it inherits the missing no-kink/boundary-regime convention.

soundness_flags.label: Related work / Exemplars
soundness_flags.one_line: Open-ended Question 4 is twice mislabeled as Conjecture 4, which blurs the proposal grammar and should be corrected.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 3 and Open-ended Questions 4-5
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Strict inclusion, finite-vertex endpoint construction, and endpoint inference are open kernels, not proved results.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: field-tier proposal conditional on resolving those open kernels, with Theorems 1-2 treated only as support results

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 2
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Connectedness of I(lambda) is not claimed; the result currently establishes compactness and endpoint monotonicity only.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: do not read Theorem 2 as proving I(lambda) equals the full interval [L(lambda),U(lambda)]

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 4
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The finite-vertex and kink construction is scoped to the locked finite support K<=5.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: no claim about larger latent supports except through the stated Caratheodory reduction convention

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define B_box as a complete endpoint program by specifying the role of pi after the shared-law and Bayes-consistency ties are dropped; then re-check the inclusion I(lambda) subset B_box(lambda).
Add a generic-regime clause or boundary convention for lambda*: state when lambda*(P)>0 exists, and what OEQ4/OEQ5 mean when the endpoint branch never switches.
Add one worked nondegenerate rational P with kappa(P) nonzero in the sanity section, even if it does not compute the full endpoint; this would make the strict-inclusion route much less promissory.

Add Frauen, Melnychuk, and Feuerriegel (2023) or another generalized sharp sensitivity paper as the named comparator for the generic sensitivity interval B_sens, or explicitly label B_sens as an internal relaxation rather than published prior art.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle; the novelty kernel survives, but the comparison-set definition and kink-regime conventions need patching before Stage 0.
